ID\n137\t\tsessionID := fmt.Sprintf(\"sim_%d\", time.Now().UnixNano())\n138\t\n139\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n140\t\t\t\"session_id\": sessionID,\n141\t\t\t\"space\":      h.space,\n142\t\t})\n143\t}\n144\t\n145\t// GetSpace returns the current space definition\n146\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetSpace(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n147\t\th.mu.RLock()\n148\t\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n149\t\n150\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, h.space)\n151\t}\n152\t\n153\t// SetSpace updates the space definition\n154\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) SetSpace(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n155\t\tvar space simulator.Space\n156\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&space); err != nil {\n157\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid space JSON\")\n158\t\t\treturn\n159\t\t}\n160\t\n161\t\tif err := space.Validate(); err != nil {\n162\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, err.Error())\n163\t\t\treturn\n164\t\t}\n165\t\n166\t\th.mu.Lock()\n167\t\th.space = &space\n168\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n169\t\n170\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{\"status\": \"updated\"})\n171\t}\n172\t\n173\t// ValidateSpace validates the current space without modifying it\n174\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) ValidateSpace(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n175\t\th.mu.RLock()\n176\t\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n177\t\n178\t\tif err := h.space.Validate(); err != nil {\n179\t\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n180\t\t\t\t\"valid\": false,\n181\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n182\t\t\t})\n183\t\t\treturn\n184\t\t}\n185\t\n186\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n187\t\t\t\"valid\":       true,\n188\t\t\t\"volume_m3\":   h.space.TotalVolume(),\n189\t\t\t\"bounds\":      getBoundsJSON(h.space),\n190\t\t\t\"room_count\":  len(h.space.Rooms),\n191\t\t\t\"wall_count\":  len(h.space.GetWalls()),\n192\t\t})\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// GetNodes returns all virtual nodes\n196\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetNodes(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n197\t\th.mu.RLock()\n198\t\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n199\t\n200\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, h.nodes.All())\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\t// AddNode adds a new virtual node\n204\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) AddNode(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n205\t\tvar node simulator.Node\n206\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&node); err != nil {\n207\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid node JSON\")\n208\t\t\treturn\n209\t\t}\n210\t\n211\t\th.mu.Lock()\n212\t\th.nodes.Add(&node)\n213\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n214\t\n215\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, node)\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// UpdateNode updates an existing node\n219\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) UpdateNode(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n220\t\tnodeID := chi.URLParam(r, \"nodeID\")\n221\t\n222\t\tvar node simulator.Node\n223\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&node); err != nil {\n224\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid node JSON\")\n225\t\t\treturn\n226\t\t}\n227\t\n228\t\tnode.ID = nodeID // Ensure ID matches URL parameter\n229\t\n230\t\th.mu.Lock()\n231\t\t// Remove old node and add updated one\n232\t\th.nodes.Remove(nodeID)\n233\t\th.nodes.Add(&node)\n234\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n235\t\n236\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, node)\n237\t}\n238\t\n239\t// RemoveNode removes a virtual node\n240\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) RemoveNode(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n241\t\tnodeID := chi.URLParam(r, \"nodeID\")\n242\t\n243\t\th.mu.Lock()\n244\t\tremoved := h.nodes.Remove(nodeID)\n245\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n246\t\n247\t\tif !removed {\n248\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, \"node not found\")\n249\t\t\treturn\n250\t\t}\n251\t\n252\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{\"status\": \"removed\"})\n253\t}\n254\t\n255\t// SuggestNodes suggests optimal node positions for the current space\n256\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) SuggestNodes(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n257\t\t// Parse count from query string\n258\t\tcountStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"count\")\n259\t\tcount, err := strconv.Atoi(countStr)\n260\t\tif err != nil || count < 1 {\n261\t\t\tcount = 4 // Default to 4 nodes\n262\t\t}\n263\t\n264\t\th.mu.RLock()\n265\t\tspace := h.space\n266\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n267\t\n268\t\tsuggested := simulator.SuggestedNodes(space, count)\n269\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, suggested.All())\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// OptimizeNodes optimizes node positions for best coverage\n273\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) OptimizeNodes(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n274\t\t// Parse parameters\n275\t\tcountStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"count\")\n276\t\tcount, err := strconv.Atoi(countStr)\n277\t\tif err != nil || count < 1 {\n278\t\t\tcount = 4\n279\t\t}\n280\t\n281\t\titerationsStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"iterations\")\n282\t\titerations, err := strconv.Atoi(iterationsStr)\n283\t\tif err != nil || iterations < 1 {\n284\t\t\titerations = 50 // Default iterations\n285\t\t}\n286\t\n287\t\th.mu.RLock()\n288\t\tspace := h.space\n289\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n290\t\n291\t\toptimized := simulator.OptimizeNodePositions(space, count, iterations)\n292\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, optimized.All())\n293\t}\n294\t\n295\t// GetWalkers returns all walkers\n296\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetWalkers(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n297\t\th.mu.RLock()\n298\t\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n299\t\n300\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, h.walkers.All())\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// AddWalker adds a new walker\n304\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) AddWalker(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n305\t\tvar walker simulator.Walker\n306\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&walker); err != nil {\n307\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid walker JSON\")\n308\t\t\treturn\n309\t\t}\n310\t\n311\t\th.mu.Lock()\n312\t\th.walkers.Add(&walker)\n313\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n314\t\n315\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, walker)\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\t// AddRandomWalkers adds random walkers to the simulation\n319\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) AddRandomWalkers(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n320\t\t// Parse count from query string\n321\t\tcountStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"count\")\n322\t\tcount, err := strconv.Atoi(countStr)\n323\t\tif err != nil || count < 1 {\n324\t\t\tcount = 1\n325\t\t}\n326\t\n327\t\th.mu.RLock()\n328\t\tspace := h.space\n329\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n330\t\n331\t\twalkers := simulator.CreateRandomWalkers(count, space)\n332\t\n333\t\th.mu.Lock()\n334\t\tfor _, w := range walkers.All() {\n335\t\t\th.walkers.Add(w)\n336\t\t}\n337\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n338\t\n339\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, walkers.All())\n340\t}\n341\t\n342\t// AddPathWalkers adds path-following walkers\n343\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) AddPathWalkers(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n344\t\t// Parse count from query string\n345\t\tcountStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"count\")\n346\t\tcount, err := strconv.Atoi(countStr)\n347\t\tif err != nil || count < 1 {\n348\t\t\tcount = 1\n349\t\t}\n350\t\n351\t\th.mu.RLock()\n352\t\tspace := h.space\n353\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n354\t\n355\t\twalkers := simulator.CreatePathWalkers(count, space)\n356\t\n357\t\th.mu.Lock()\n358\t\tfor _, w := range walkers.All() {\n359\t\t\th.walkers.Add(w)\n360\t\t}\n361\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n362\t\n363\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, walkers.All())\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t// RemoveWalker removes a walker\n367\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) RemoveWalker(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n368\t\twalkerID := chi.URLParam(r, \"walkerID\")\n369\t\n370\t\th.mu.Lock()\n371\t\tremoved := h.walkers.Remove(walkerID)\n372\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n373\t\n374\t\tif !removed {\n375\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, \"walker not found\")\n376\t\t\treturn\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{\"status\": \"removed\"})\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t// GDOPRequest contains parameters for GDOP computation\n383\ttype GDOPRequest struct {\n384\t\tCellSize  float64 `json:\"cell_size\"`  // Grid cell size in meters\n385\t\tMaxZone   int     `json:\"max_zone\"`   // Maximum Fresnel zone to consider\n386\t\tThreshold float64 `json:\"threshold\"`  // DeltaRMS threshold for active links\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// GDOPResponse contains GDOP computation results\n390\ttype GDOPResponse struct {\n391\t\tResults        [][]simulator.GDOPResult `json:\"results\"`\n392\t\tCoverageScore  float64                   `json:\"coverage_score\"`\n393\t\tAverageGDOP    float64                   `json:\"average_gdop\"`\n394\t\tQualityCounts  map[string]int            `json:\"quality_counts\"`\n395\t\tDeadZones      []simulator.Point         `json:\"dead_zones\"`\n396\t\tRecommendedPos simulator.Point          `json:\"recommended_position\"`\n397\t\tLinks          []simulator.Link          `json:\"links\"`\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// ComputeGDOP computes GDOP for the current configuration\n401\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) ComputeGDOP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n402\t\tvar req GDOPRequest\n403\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n404\t\t\t// Use defaults if request body is empty\n405\t\t\treq = GDOPRequest{\n406\t\t\t\tCellSize:  0.2,\n407\t\t\t\tMaxZone:   3,\n408\t\t\t\tThreshold: 0.02,\n409\t\t\t}\n410\t\t}\n411\t\n412\t\th.mu.RLock()\n413\t\tspace := h.space\n414\t\tnodes := h.nodes\n415\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n416\t\n417\t\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n418\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"need at least 2 nodes for GDOP computation\")\n419\t\t\treturn\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n423\t\n424\t\t// Generate links\n425\t\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n426\t\n427\t\t// Create GDOP computer\n428\t\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n429\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n430\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n431\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n432\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n433\t\t\tCellSize: req.CellSize,\n434\t\t}\n435\t\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n436\t\tif req.MaxZone > 0 {\n437\t\t\tgdopComp.SetMaxZone(req.MaxZone)\n438\t\t}\n439\t\n440\t\t// Compute GDOP\n441\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n442\t\n443\t\t// Compute statistics\n444\t\tcoverageScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n445\t\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n446\t\tqualityCounts := gdopComp.QualityCounts(results)\n447\t\tdeadZones := gdopComp.FindDeadZones(results)\n448\t\trecommendedPos := gdopComp.RecommendNodePosition(results, space)\n449\t\n450\t\tresponse := GDOPResponse{\n451\t\t\tResults:        results,\n452\t\t\tCoverageScore:  coverageScore,\n453\t\t\tAverageGDOP:    avgGDOP,\n454\t\t\tQualityCounts:  qualityCounts,\n455\t\t\tDeadZones:      deadZones,\n456\t\t\tRecommendedPos: recommendedPos,\n457\t\t\tLinks:          links,\n458\t\t}\n459\t\n460\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n461\t}\n462\t\n463\t// GetCoverageScore returns just the coverage score for quick assessment\n464\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetCoverageScore(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n465\t\th.mu.RLock()\n466\t\tspace := h.space\n467\t\tnodes := h.nodes\n468\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n469\t\n470\t\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n471\t\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n472\t\t\t\t\"coverage_percent\": 0,\n473\t\t\t\t\"minimum_nodes\":    simulator.MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n474\t\t\t\t\"current_nodes\":    nodes.Count(),\n475\t\t\t})\n476\t\t\treturn\n477\t\t}\n478\t\n479\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n480\t\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n481\t\n482\t\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n483\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n484\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n485\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n486\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n487\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n488\t\t}\n489\t\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n490\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n491\t\n492\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n493\t\t\t\"coverage_percent\": gdopComp.CoverageScore(results),\n494\t\t\t\"minimum_nodes\":    simulator.MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n495\t\t\t\"current_nodes\":    nodes.Count(),\n496\t\t\t\"average_gdop\":     gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results),\n497\t\t})\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t// GetGDOPHeatmap returns GDOP data in a format suitable for heatmap visualization\n501\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetGDOPHeatmap(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n502\t\th.mu.RLock()\n503\t\tspace := h.space\n504\t\tnodes := h.nodes\n505\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n506\t\n507\t\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n508\t\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n509\t\t\t\t\"gdop_map\":         []float64{},\n510\t\t\t\t\"grid_dimensions\": []int{0, 0, 0},\n511\t\t\t\t\"coverage_percent\": 0,\n512\t\t\t\t\"error\":            \"need at least 2 nodes\",\n513\t\t\t})\n514\t\t\treturn\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n518\t\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n519\t\n520\t\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n521\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n522\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n523\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n524\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n525\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n526\t\t}\n527\t\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n528\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n529\t\n530\t\t// Convert results to heatmap format\n531\t\tdepth := len(results)\n532\t\twidth := 0\n533\t\tif depth > 0 {\n534\t\t\twidth = len(results[0])\n535\t\t}\n536\t\n537\t\t// Flatten GDOP values into 1D array (row-major order)\n538\t\tgdopMap := make([]float64, width*depth)\n539\t\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n540\t\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n541\t\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n542\t\t\t\tif math.IsInf(results[y][x].GDOP, 0) {\n543\t\t\t\t\tgdopMap[idx] = 9999.0 // Use 9999 to represent infinity\n544\t\t\t\t} else {\n545\t\t\t\t\tgdopMap[idx] = results[y][x].GDOP\n546\t\t\t\t}\n547\t\t\t}\n548\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n551\t\t\t\"gdop_map\":         gdopMap,\n552\t\t\t\"grid_dimensions\": []int{width, depth, 1}, // 2D heatmap, so height = 1\n553\t\t\t\"coverage_percent\": gdopComp.CoverageScore(results),\n554\t\t\t\"average_gdop\":     gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results),\n555\t\t\t\"quality_counts\":   gdopComp.QualityCounts(results),\n556\t\t})\n557\t}\n558\t\n559\t// GetShoppingList returns hardware recommendations\n560\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetShoppingList(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n561\t\th.mu.RLock()\n562\t\tspace := h.space\n563\t\tnodes := h.nodes\n564\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n565\t\n566\t\tshoppingList := simulator.GenerateShoppingList(space, nodes)\n567\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, shoppingList)\n568\t}\n569\t\n570\t// SimulationRequest contains parameters for running a simulation\n571\ttype SimulationRequest struct {\n572\t\tDurationSec int     `json:\"duration_sec\"` // Simulation duration in seconds\n573\t\tRateHz      int     `json:\"rate_hz\"`      // Update rate in Hz\n574\t\tThreshold   float64 `json:\"threshold\"`    // DeltaRMS threshold\n575\t}\n576\t\n577\t// SimulationResponse contains simulation results\n578\ttype SimulationResponse struct {\n579\t\tWalkerPositions []simulator.Point `json:\"walker_positions\"`\n580\t\tLinkActivity    map[string]float64 `json:\"link_activity\"`\n581\t\tDuration        int                `json:\"duration\"`\n582\t\tTicks           int                `json:\"ticks\"`\n583\t}\n584\t\n585\t// RunSimulation runs a time-step simulation with the current configuration\n586\tfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) RunSimulation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n587\t\tvar req SimulationRequest\n588\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n589\t\t\treq = SimulationRequest{\n590\t\t\t\tDurationSec: 10,\n591\t\t\t\tRateHz:      10,\n592\t\t\t\tThreshold:   0.02,\n593\t\t\t}\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\th.mu.RLock()\n597\t\tspace := h.space\n598\t\tnodes := h.nodes\n599\t\twalkers := h.walkers\n600\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n601\t\n602\t\tif walkers.Count() == 0 {\n603\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"no walkers in simulation\")\n604\t\t\treturn\n605\t\t}\n606\t\n607\t\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n608\t\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"need at least 2 nodes for simulation\")\n609\t\t\treturn\n610\t\t}\n611\t\n612\t\t// Create propagation model\n613\t\tpropModel := simulator.NewPropagationModel(space)\n614\t\n615\t\t// Generate all links\n616\t\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n617\t\n618\t\t// Run simulation ticks\n619\t\tdt := 1.0 / float64(req.RateHz)\n620\t\tnumTicks := req.DurationSec * req.RateHz\n621\t\n622\t\t// Collect final positions and link activity\n623\t\tfinalPositions := make([]simulator.Point, 0)\n624\t\tlinkActivity := make(map[string]float64)\n625\t\n626\t\tfor tick := 0; tick < numTicks; tick++ {\n627\t\t\t// Update walker positions\n628\t\t\twalkers.Update(dt, space)\n629\t\n630\t\t\t// Compute link activity\n631\t\t\tfor _, link := range links {\n632\t\t\t\tlinkID := link.TX.ID + \":\" + link.RX.ID\n633\t\t\t\tmaxDelta := 0.0\n634\t\n635\t\t\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers.All() {\n636\t\t\t\t\tdelta := propModel.ComputeLinkActivity(link, walker.Position, req.Threshold)\n637\t\t\t\t\tif delta > maxDelta {\n638\t\t\t\t\t\tmaxDelta = delta\n639\t\t\t\t\t}\n640\t\t\t\t}\n641\t\n642\t\t\t\tif maxDelta > linkActivity[linkID] {\n643\t\t\t\t\tlinkActivity[linkID] = maxDelta\n644\t\t\t\t}\n645\t\t\t}\n646\t\t}\n647\t\n648\t\t// Collect final walker positions\n649\t\tfor _, w := range walkers.All() {\n650\t\t\tfinalPositions = append(finalPositions, w.Position)\n651\t\t}\n652\t\n653\t\tresponse := SimulationResponse{\n654\t\t\tWalkerPositions: finalPositions,\n655\t\t\tLinkActivity:    linkActivity,\n656\t\t\tDuration:        req.DurationSec,\n657\t\t\tTicks:           numTicks,\n658\t\t}\n659\t\n660\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n661\t}\n662\t\n663\t// Helper functions\n664\t\n665\tfunc getBoundsJSON(space *simulator.Space) map[string]float64 {\n666\t\tminX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n667\t\treturn map[string]float64{\n668\t\t\t\"min_x\": minX,\n669\t\t\t\"min_y\": minY,\n670\t\t\t\"min_z\": minZ,\n671\t\t\t\"max_x\": maxX,\n672\t\t\t\"max_y\": maxY,\n673\t\t\t\"max_z\": maxZ,\n674\t\t}\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\tfunc respondJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, data interface{}) {\n678\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n679\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n680\t\tif err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(data); err != nil {\n681\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to encode JSON response: %v\", err)\n682\t\t}\n683\t}\n684\t\n685\tfunc respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, message string) {\n686\t\trespondJSON(w, status, map[string]string{\"error\": {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tstate := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"space\":   h.space,\n\t\t\"nodes\":   h.nodes.All(),\n\t\t\"walkers\": h.walkers.All(),\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, simulation\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) AddRandomWalkers(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t// Parse count from query string\n\tcountStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"count\")\n\tcount, err := map[string]string{\"status\": \"removed\"})\n}\n\n// GDOPRequest contains parameters for GDOP computation\ntype GDOPRequest struct {\n\tCellSize  float64 `json:\"cell_size\"`  // Grid cell size in GDOP computation results\ntype GDOPResponse struct {\n\tResults        [][]simulator.GDOPResult `json:\"results\"`\n\tCoverageScore  float64                   `json:\"coverage_score\"`\n\tAverageGDOP    float64                   `json:\"average_gdop\"`\n\tQualityCounts  map[string]int            `json:\"quality_counts\"`\n\tDeadZones      []simulator.Point         `json:\"dead_zones\"`\n\tRecommendedPos simulator.Point          `json:\"recommended_position\"`\n\tLinks          []simulator.Link          `json:\"links\"`\n}\n\n// ComputeGDOP computes GDOP for the current configuration\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) ComputeGDOP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req GDOPRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\t// Use defaults if request body is empty\n\t\treq = GDOPRequest{\n\t\t\tCellSize:  0.2,\n\t\t\tMaxZone:   3,\n\t\t\tThreshold: 0.02,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"need at least 2 nodes for GDOP computation\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\n\t// Generate links\n\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\n\t// Create GDOP computer\n\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: req.CellSize,\n\t}\n\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n\tif req.MaxZone > 0 {\n\t\tgdopComp.SetMaxZone(req.MaxZone)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute GDOP\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\t// Compute statistics\n\tcoverageScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n\tqualityCounts := gdopComp.QualityCounts(results)\n\tdeadZones := gdopComp.FindDeadZones(results)\n\trecommendedPos := gdopComp.RecommendNodePosition(results, space)\n\n\tresponse := GDOPResponse{\n\t\tResults:        results,\n\t\tCoverageScore:  coverageScore,\n\t\tAverageGDOP:    avgGDOP,\n\t\tQualityCounts:  qualityCounts,\n\t\tDeadZones:      deadZones,\n\t\tRecommendedPos: recommendedPos,\n\t\tLinks:          links,\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// GetCoverageScore returns just the coverage score for quick assessment\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetCoverageScore(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"coverage_percent\": 0,\n\t\t\t\"minimum_nodes\":    simulator.MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n\t\t\t\"current_nodes\":    nodes.Count(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\n\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t}\n\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"coverage_percent\": gdopComp.CoverageScore(results),\n\t\t\"minimum_nodes\":    simulator.MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n\t\t\"current_nodes\":    nodes.Count(),\n\t\t\"average_gdop\":     gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results),\n\t})\n}\n\n// GetGDOPHeatmap returns GDOP data in a format suitable for heatmap visualization\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetGDOPHeatmap(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"gdop_map\":         []float64{},\n\t\t\t\"grid_dimensions\": []int{0, 0, 0},\n\t\t\t\"coverage_percent\": 0,\n\t\t\t\"error\":            \"need at least 2 nodes\",\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\n\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t}\n\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\t// Convert results to heatmap format\n\tdepth := len(results)\n\twidth := 0\n\tif depth > 0 {\n\t\twidth = len(results[0])\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten GDOP values into 1D array (row-major order)\n\tgdopMap := make([]float64, width*depth)\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tif math.IsInf(results[y][x].GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tgdopMap[idx] = 9999.0 // Use 9999 to represent infinity\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tgdopMap[idx] = results[y][x].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"gdop_map\":         gdopMap,\n\t\t\"grid_dimensions\": []int{width, depth, 1}, // 2D heatmap, so height = 1\n\t\t\"coverage_percent\": gdopComp.CoverageScore(results),\n\t\t\"average_gdop\":     gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results),\n\t\t\"quality_counts\":   gdopComp.QualityCounts(results),\n\t})\n}\n\n// GetShoppingList returns hardware recommendations\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetShoppingList(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tshoppingList := simulator.GenerateShoppingList(space, nodes)\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, shoppingList)\n}\n\n// SimulationRequest contains parameters for running a simulation\ntype SimulationRequest struct {\n\tDurationSec int     `json:\"duration_sec\"` // Simulation duration in seconds\n\tRateHz      int     `json:\"rate_hz\"`      // Update rate in Hz\n\tThreshold   float64 `json:\"threshold\"`    // DeltaRMS threshold\n}\n\n// SimulationResponse contains simulation results\ntype SimulationResponse struct {\n\tWalkerPositions []simulator.Point `json:\"walker_positions\"`\n\tLinkActivity    map[string]float64 `json:\"link_activity\"`\n\tDuration        int                `json:\"duration\"`\n\tTicks           int                `json:\"ticks\"`\n}\n\n// RunSimulation runs a time-step simulation with the current configuration\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) RunSimulation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req SimulationRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\treq = SimulationRequest{\n\t\t\tDurationSec: 10,\n\t\t\tRateHz:      10,\n\t\t\tThreshold:   0.02,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\twalkers := h.walkers\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif walkers.Count() == 0 {\n\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"no walkers in simulation\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"need at least 2 nodes for simulation\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create propagation model\n\tpropModel := simulator.NewPropagationModel(space)\n\n\t// Generate all links\n\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\n\t// Run simulation ticks\n\tdt := 1.0 / float64(req.RateHz)\n\tnumTicks := req.DurationSec * req.RateHz\n\n\t// Collect final positions and link activity\n\tfinalPositions := make([]simulator.Point, 0)\n\tlinkActivity := make(map[string]float64)\n\n\tfor tick := 0; tick < numTicks; tick++ {\n\t\t// Update walker positions\n\t\twalkers.Update(dt, space)\n\n\t\t// Compute link activity\n\t\tfor _, link := range links {\n\t\t\tlinkID := link.TX.ID + \":\" + link.RX.ID\n\t\t\tmaxDelta := 0.0\n\n\t\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers.All() {\n\t\t\t\tdelta := propModel.ComputeLinkActivity(link, walker.Position, req.Threshold)\n\t\t\t\tif delta > maxDelta {\n\t\t\t\t\tmaxDelta = delta\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif maxDelta > linkActivity[linkID] {\n\t\t\t\tlinkActivity[linkID] = maxDelta\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Collect final walker positions\n\tfor _, w := range walkers.All() {\n\t\tfinalPositions = append(finalPositions, w.Position)\n\t}\n\n\tresponse := SimulationResponse{\n\t\tWalkerPositions: finalPositions,\n\t\tLinkActivity:    linkActivity,\n\t\tDuration:        req.DurationSec,\n\t\tTicks:           numTicks,\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// Helper functions\n\nfunc getBoundsJSON(space *simulator.Space) map[string]float64 {\n\tminX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\treturn map[string]float64{\n\t\t\"min_x\": minX,\n\t\t\"min_y\": minY,\n\t\t\"min_z\": minZ,\n\t\t\"max_x\": maxX,\n\t\t\"max_y\": maxY,\n\t\t\"max_z\": maxZ,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc respondJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, data interface{}) {\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\tif err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(data); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to encode JSON response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, message string) {\n\trespondJSON(w, status, (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"math\"\n6\t\tmrand \"math/rand\"\n7\t)\n8\t\n9\t// GDOPResult contains GDOP computation results for a single cell\n10\ttype GDOPResult struct {\n11\t\tX, Y, Z      float64  // Cell center position\n12\t\tGDOP         float64  // Computed GDOP value (Infinity = no coverage)\n13\t\tQuality      string   // \"excellent\", \"good\", \"fair\", \"poor\", \"none\"\n14\t\tContributingLinks []string // Link IDs that contributed to this cell\n15\t}\n16\t\n17\t// GridConfig defines the GDOP computation grid\n18\ttype GridConfig struct {\n19\t\tCellSize    float64 // Grid cell size in meters\n20\t\tMinX, MinY  float64 // Grid origin\n21\t\tWidth       float64 // Grid width\n22\t\tDepth       float64 // Grid depth\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// GDOPComputer computes Geometric Dilution of Precision for coverage analysis\n26\ttype GDOPComputer struct {\n27\t\tlinks     []Link\n28\t\tconfig    GridConfig\n29\t\tmaxZone   int // Maximum Fresnel zone to consider (default 3)\n30\t}\n31\t\n32\t// NewGDOPComputer creates a new GDOP computer\n33\tfunc NewGDOPComputer(links []Link, config GridConfig) *GDOPComputer {\n34\t\tif config.CellSize <= 0 {\n35\t\t\tconfig.CellSize = 0.2 // Default 20cm\n36\t\t}\n37\t\treturn &GDOPComputer{\n38\t\t\tlinks:   links,\n39\t\t\tconfig:  config,\n40\t\t\tmaxZone: 3, // Default: consider first 3 Fresnel zones\n41\t\t}\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\t// SetMaxZone sets the maximum Fresnel zone to consider\n45\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) SetMaxZone(zone int) {\n46\t\tif zone < 1 {\n47\t\t\tzone = 1\n48\t\t}\n49\t\tgc.maxZone = zone\n50\t}\n51\t\n52\t// ComputeAll computes GDOP for the entire grid\n53\t// Returns a slice of GDOP results indexed by cell position\n54\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAll() [][]GDOPResult {\n55\t\tnx := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Width / gc.config.CellSize))\n56\t\tny := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Depth / gc.config.CellSize))\n57\t\n58\t\tresults := make([][]GDOPResult, ny)\n59\t\n60\t\tfor iy := 0; iy < ny; iy++ {\n61\t\t\tresults[iy] = make([]GDOPResult, nx)\n62\t\t\tfor ix := 0; ix < nx; ix++ {\n63\t\t\t\tx := gc.config.MinX + (float64(ix)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n64\t\t\t\ty := gc.config.MinY + (float64(iy)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n65\t\t\t\tz := 1.0 // Use 1m height for 2D GDOP analysis\n66\t\n67\t\t\t\tresult := gc.ComputeAt(x, y, z)\n68\t\t\t\tresults[iy][ix] = result\n69\t\t\t}\n70\t\t}\n71\t\n72\t\treturn results\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\t// ComputeAt computes GDOP at a specific point\n76\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAt(x, y, z float64) GDOPResult {\n77\t\tpoint := Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: z}\n78\t\n79\t\t// Collect links that cover this point (within maxZone Fresnel zones)\n80\t\tvar coveringLinks []Link\n81\t\tvar linkIDs []string\n82\t\n83\t\tfor _, link := range gc.links {\n84\t\t\tif IsInFresnelZones(link.TX.Position, link.RX.Position, point, gc.maxZone) {\n85\t\t\t\tcoveringLinks = append(coveringLinks, link)\n86\t\t\t\tlinkIDs = append(linkIDs, link.TX.ID+\":\"+link.RX.ID)\n87\t\t\t}\n88\t\t}\n89\t\n90\t\tresult := GDOPResult{\n91\t\t\tX:                 x,\n92\t\t\tY:                 y,\n93\t\t\tZ:                 z,\n94\t\t\tContributingLinks: linkIDs,\n95\t\t}\n96\t\n97\t\tif len(coveringLinks) < 2 {\n98\t\t\t// Need at least 2 links for 2D localization\n99\t\t\tresult.GDOP = math.Inf(1)\n100\t\t\tresult.Quality = \"none\"\n101\t\t\treturn result\n102\t\t}\n103\t\n104\t\t// Compute GDOP using angular diversity\n105\t\tgdop := gc.computeGDOPAngular(point, coveringLinks)\n106\t\tresult.GDOP = gdop\n107\t\tresult.Quality = gdopToQuality(gdop)\n108\t\n109\t\treturn result\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t// computeGDOPAngular computes GDOP based on angular diversity of link directions\n113\t// This is the 2D GDOP formula from the plan\n114\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) computeGDOPAngular(point Point, links []Link) float64 {\n115\t\t// Step 1: Collect link angles\n116\t\t// For each link, compute the angle of the line from TX to RX as seen from point\n117\t\ttype linkAngle struct {\n118\t\t\ttheta float64 // angle in radians\n119\t\t\tlink  Link\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\tangles := make([]linkAngle, 0, len(links))\n123\t\tfor _, link := range links {\n124\t\t\t// Project to floor plane (ignore Z for 2D analysis)\n125\t\t\tdx := link.RX.Position.X - link.TX.Position.X\n126\t\t\tdy := link.RX.Position.Y - link.TX.Position.Y\n127\t\t\ttheta := math.Atan2(dy, dx)\n128\t\t\tangles = append(angles, linkAngle{theta: theta, link: link})\n129\t\t}\n130\t\n131\t\tif len(angles) < 2 {\n132\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n133\t\t}\n134\t\n135\t\t// Step 2: Build Fisher information matrix\n136\t\t// F = Σ [ [cos²(θ),       cos(θ)·sin(θ)],\n137\t\t//        [cos(θ)·sin(θ), sin²(θ)       ] ]\n138\t\tvar f00, f01, f11 float64\n139\t\n140\t\tfor _, la := range angles {\n141\t\t\tc := math.Cos(la.theta)\n142\t\t\ts := math.Sin(la.theta)\n143\t\n144\t\t\tf00 += c * c\n145\t\t\tf01 += c * s\n146\t\t\tf11 += s * s\n147\t\t}\n148\t\n149\t\t// Step 3: Compute determinant\n150\t\tdet := f00*f11 - f01*f01\n151\t\n152\t\t// Check for degenerate geometry (collinear links)\n153\t\tif det <= 1e-6 {\n154\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n155\t\t}\n156\t\n157\t\t// Step 4: Compute trace of F^-1\n158\t\t// For 2x2 matrix: trace(F^-1) = (f00 + f11) / det\n159\t\ttraceFInv := (f00 + f11) / det\n160\t\n161\t\t// Step 5: GDOP = sqrt(trace(F^-1))\n162\t\tgdop := math.Sqrt(traceFInv)\n163\t\n164\t\treturn gdop\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// gdopToQuality converts GDOP value to quality string\n168\tfunc gdopToQuality(gdop float64) string {\n169\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n170\t\t\treturn \"none\"\n171\t\t}\n172\t\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n173\t\t\treturn \"excellent\"\n174\t\t}\n175\t\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n176\t\t\treturn \"good\"\n177\t\t}\n178\t\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n179\t\t\treturn \"fair\"\n180\t\t}\n181\t\treturn \"poor\"\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// CoverageScore computes the percentage of cells with \"good\" or better coverage\n185\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) CoverageScore(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n186\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n187\t\t\treturn 0\n188\t\t}\n189\t\n190\t\tgoodCells := 0\n191\t\ttotalCells := 0\n192\t\n193\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n194\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n195\t\t\t\ttotalCells++\n196\t\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"excellent\" || cell.Quality == \"good\" {\n197\t\t\t\t\tgoodCells++\n198\t\t\t\t}\n199\t\t\t}\n200\t\t}\n201\t\n202\t\treturn 100.0 * float64(goodCells) / float64(totalCells)\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t// AverageGDOP computes the average GDOP over all cells (excluding infinity)\n206\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) AverageGDOP(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n207\t\tsum := 0.0\n208\t\tcount := 0\n209\t\n210\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n211\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n212\t\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(cell.GDOP, 0) {\n213\t\t\t\t\tsum += cell.GDOP\n214\t\t\t\t\tcount++\n215\t\t\t\t}\n216\t\t\t}\n217\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\tif count == 0 {\n220\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n221\t\t}\n222\t\treturn sum / float64(count)\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\t// QualityCounts returns the count of cells by quality level\n226\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) QualityCounts(results [][]GDOPResult) map[string]int {\n227\t\tcounts := map[string]int{\n228\t\t\t\"excellent\": 0,\n229\t\t\t\"good\":      0,\n230\t\t\t\"fair\":      0,\n231\t\t\t\"poor\":      0,\n232\t\t\t\"none\":      0,\n233\t\t}\n234\t\n235\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n236\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n237\t\t\t\tcounts[cell.Quality]++\n238\t\t\t}\n239\t\t}\n240\t\n241\t\treturn counts\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t// FindDeadZones returns positions where coverage is \"none\" or \"poor\"\n245\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) FindDeadZones(results [][]GDOPResult) []Point {\n246\t\tdeadZones := make([]Point, 0)\n247\t\n248\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n249\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n250\t\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"none\" || cell.Quality == \"poor\" {\n251\t\t\t\t\tdeadZones = append(deadZones, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n252\t\t\t\t}\n253\t\t\t}\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn deadZones\n257\t}\n258\t\n259\t// RecommendNodePosition suggests optimal positions for additional nodes\n260\t// based on current dead zones\n261\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) RecommendNodePosition(results [][]GDOPResult, space *Space) Point {\n262\t\t// Find the centroid of the largest dead zone\n263\t\tdeadZones := gc.FindDeadZones(results)\n264\t\tif len(deadZones) == 0 {\n265\t\t\t// No dead zones, suggest center of space\n266\t\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n267\t\t\treturn Point{\n268\t\t\t\tX: (minX + maxX) / 2,\n269\t\t\t\tY: (minY + maxY) / 2,\n270\t\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement\n271\t\t\t}\n272\t\t}\n273\t\n274\t\t// Cluster dead zones and find the largest cluster\n275\t\t// Simplified: just return the centroid of all dead zones\n276\t\tvar sumX, sumY float64\n277\t\tfor _, dz := range deadZones {\n278\t\t\tsumX += dz.X\n279\t\t\tsumY += dz.Y\n280\t\t}\n281\t\n282\t\tcentroid := Point{\n283\t\t\tX: sumX / float64(len(deadZones)),\n284\t\t\tY: sumY / float64(len(deadZones)),\n285\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement for better coverage\n286\t\t}\n287\t\n288\t\treturn centroid\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t// MinimumNodeCount estimates the minimum number of nodes needed for good coverage\n292\t// Based on space dimensions and desired quality threshold\n293\tfunc MinimumNodeCount(space *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n294\t\twidth, depth, _ := space.Dimensions()\n295\t\tarea := width * depth\n296\t\n297\t\t// Heuristic: nodes needed based on area and desired GDOP\n298\t\t// For GDOP < 4 (good coverage): approximately 1 node per 15-20 m²\n299\t\tif targetGDOP < 2 {\n300\t\t\t// Excellent coverage requires more nodes\n301\t\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 15.0))\n302\t\t}\n303\t\tif targetGDOP < 4 {\n304\t\t\t// Good coverage\n305\t\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 20.0))\n306\t\t}\n307\t\t// Fair coverage\n308\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0))\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// ExpectedAccuracy estimates the expected localization accuracy at a point\n312\t// based on its GDOP value\n313\tfunc ExpectedAccuracy(gdop float64) float64 {\n314\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n315\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t// Based on research: typical CSI accuracy with 4+ nodes is ±0.5-1.0m\n319\t\t// GDOP < 2: ±0.5m, GDOP 2-4: ±1.0m, GDOP > 4: degrades further\n320\t\tbaseAccuracy := 0.5 // meters for GDOP = 1\n321\t\n322\t\treturn baseAccuracy * gdop\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t// GDOPColor represents a color for GDOP visualization\n326\ttype GDOPColor struct {\n327\t\tR, G, B uint8 // RGB values 0-255\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t// GDOPColorMap returns the color for a given GDOP value for visualization\n331\t// Uses: green (excellent), yellow (good), orange (fair), red (poor), gray (none)\n332\tfunc GDOPColorMap(gdop float64) GDOPColor {\n333\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n334\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 80, G: 80, B: 80} // Gray for no coverage\n335\t\t}\n336\t\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n337\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 34, G: 197, B: 94} // Green (#22c65e) for excellent\n338\t\t}\n339\t\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n340\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 193, B: 7} // Yellow (#ffc107) for good\n341\t\t}\n342\t\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n343\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 146, B: 0} // Orange (#ff9200) for fair\n344\t\t}\n345\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 220, G: 53, B: 69} // Red (#dc3545) for poor\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// GDOPHeatmapData represents flattened GDOP data for frontend rendering\n349\ttype GDOPHeatmapData struct {\n350\t\tWidth      int         `json:\"width\"`       // Grid width (columns)\n351\t\tDepth      int         `json:\"depth\"`       // Grid depth (rows)\n352\t\tCellSize   float64     `json:\"cell_size\"`   // Cell size in meters\n353\t\tOriginX    float64     `json:\"origin_x\"`    // Grid origin X\n354\t\tOriginY    float64     `json:\"origin_y\"`    // Grid origin Y\n355\t\tGDOPValues []float64   `json:\"gdop_values\"` // Flattened GDOP values (9999 = infinity)\n356\t\tQualities  []string    `json:\"qualities\"`   // Flattened quality strings\n357\t\tColors     [][]uint8   `json:\"colors\"`      // Flattened RGB colors [width*depth*3]\n358\t\tAccuracyMap []float64   `json:\"accuracy_map\"` // Expected accuracy in meters per cell\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t// ToHeatmapData converts GDOP results to a heatmap-friendly format\n362\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ToHeatmapData(results [][]GDOPResult) *GDOPHeatmapData {\n363\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n364\t\t\treturn &GDOPHeatmapData{}\n365\t\t}\n366\t\n367\t\tdepth := len(results)    // rows (Y)\n368\t\twidth := len(results[0]) // cols (X)\n369\t\ttotalCells := width * depth\n370\t\n371\t\tdata := &GDOPHeatmapData{\n372\t\t\tWidth:       width,\n373\t\t\tDepth:       depth,\n374\t\t\tCellSize:    gc.config.CellSize,\n375\t\t\tOriginX:     gc.config.MinX,\n376\t\t\tOriginY:     gc.config.MinY,\n377\t\t\tGDOPValues:  make([]float64, totalCells),\n378\t\t\tQualities:   make([]string, totalCells),\n379\t\t\tColors:      make([][]uint8, totalCells),\n380\t\t\tAccuracyMap: make([]float64, totalCells),\n381\t\t}\n382\t\n383\t\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n384\t\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n385\t\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n386\t\t\t\tresult := results[y][x]\n387\t\n388\t\t\t\t// GDOP value (9999 for infinity)\n389\t\t\t\tif math.IsInf(result.GDOP, 0) {\n390\t\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = 9999.0\n391\t\t\t\t} else {\n392\t\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = result.GDOP\n393\t\t\t\t}\n394\t\n395\t\t\t\t// Quality string\n396\t\t\t\tdata.Qualities[idx] = result.Quality\n397\t\n398\t\t\t\t// RGB color\n399\t\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(result.GDOP)\n400\t\t\t\tdata.Colors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n401\t\n402\t\t\t\t// Expected accuracy\n403\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = ExpectedAccuracy(result.GDOP)\n404\t\t\t}\n405\t\t}\n406\t\n407\t\treturn data\n408\t}\n409\t\n410\t// ComputeAccuracyMap computes expected accuracy for each cell\n411\t// Returns a 2D array of accuracy values in meters (infinity = no coverage)\n412\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAccuracyMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]float64 {\n413\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n414\t\t\treturn nil\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\taccuracyMap := make([][]float64, len(results))\n418\t\tfor i := range results {\n419\t\t\taccuracyMap[i] = make([]float64, len(results[i]))\n420\t\t\tfor j := range results[i] {\n421\t\t\t\taccuracyMap[i][j] = ExpectedAccuracy(results[i][j].GDOP)\n422\t\t\t}\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\treturn accuracyMap\n426\t}\n427\t\n428\t// ComputeColorMap computes RGB colors for each cell for visualization\n429\t// Returns a flattened array of RGB values [width*depth*3]\n430\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeColorMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]uint8 {\n431\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n432\t\t\treturn nil\n433\t\t}\n434\t\n435\t\tdepth := len(results)\n436\t\twidth := len(results[0])\n437\t\ttotalCells := width * depth\n438\t\n439\t\tcolors := make([][]uint8, totalCells)\n440\t\n441\t\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n442\t\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n443\t\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n444\t\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(results[y][x].GDOP)\n445\t\t\t\tcolors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n446\t\t\t}\n447\t\t}\n448\t\n449\t\treturn colors\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t// GetWorstCoverageCells returns the N cells with the worst GDOP values\n453\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetWorstCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n454\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n455\t\t\treturn nil\n456\t\t}\n457\t\n458\t\t// Flatten all cells\n459\t\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n460\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n461\t\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n462\t\t}\n463\t\n464\t\t// Sort by GDOP (descending, so worst first)\n465\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n466\t\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n467\t\t\t\t// Handle infinity: infinity is worse than any finite value\n468\t\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n469\t\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n470\t\n471\t\t\t\tvar swap bool\n472\t\t\t\tif iInf && !jInf {\n473\t\t\t\t\tswap = false // i stays (infinity at top)\n474\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && jInf {\n475\t\t\t\t\tswap = true // j is infinity, should be before i\n476\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n477\t\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP > cells[i].GDOP\n478\t\t\t\t}\n479\t\n480\t\t\t\tif swap {\n481\t\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n482\t\t\t\t}\n483\t\t\t}\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\t// Return top N worst cells\n487\t\tif n > len(cells) {\n488\t\t\tn = len(cells)\n489\t\t}\n490\t\treturn cells[:n]\n491\t}\n492\t\n493\t// GetBestCoverageCells returns the N cells with the best GDOP values\n494\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetBestCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n495\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n496\t\t\treturn nil\n497\t\t}\n498\t\n499\t\t// Flatten all cells\n500\t\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n501\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n502\t\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n503\t\t}\n504\t\n505\t\t// Sort by GDOP (ascending, so best first)\n506\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n507\t\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n508\t\t\t\t// Handle infinity: finite values are better than infinity\n509\t\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n510\t\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n511\t\n512\t\t\t\tvar swap bool\n513\t\t\t\tif !iInf && jInf {\n514\t\t\t\t\tswap = false // i is finite, j is infinity, i is better\n515\t\t\t\t} else if iInf && !jInf {\n516\t\t\t\t\tswap = true // i is infinity, j is finite, j should be before i\n517\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n518\t\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP < cells[i].GDOP\n519\t\t\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\t\t\tif swap {\n522\t\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n523\t\t\t\t}\n524\t\t\t}\n525\t\t}\n526\t\n527\t\t// Return top N best cells\n528\t\tif n > len(cells) {\n529\t\t\tn = len(cells)\n530\t\t}\n531\t\treturn cells[:n]\n532\t}\n533\t\n534\t// OptimizeNodePositions uses a greedy algorithm to find better node positions\n535\t// for a given number of nodes within the space\n536\tfunc OptimizeNodePositions(space *Space, numNodes int, iterations int) *NodeSet {\n537\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n538\t\n539\t\t// Start with corner positions\n540\t\tbestSet := NewNodeSet()\n541\t\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n542\t\n543\t\tfor i := 0; i < numNodes; i++ {\n544\t\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n545\t\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n546\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n547\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n548\t\t\t\t\tcorners[i],\n549\t\t\t\t)\n550\t\t\t} else {\n551\t\t\t\t// Add random position (Go 1.20+ automatically seeds global generator)\n552\t\t\t\tpos := Point{\n553\t\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n554\t\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n555\t\t\t\t\tZ: mrand.Float64() * maxZ,\n556\t\t\t\t}\n557\t\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n558\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n559\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n560\t\t\t\t\tpos,\n561\t\t\t\t)\n562\t\t\t}\n563\t\t}\n564\t\n565\t\t// Generate initial links and compute coverage\n566\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(bestSet)\n567\t\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n568\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n569\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n570\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n571\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n572\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n573\t\t})\n574\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n575\t\tbestScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n576\t\n577\t\t// Iterative improvement\n578\t\tfor iter := 0; iter < iterations; iter++ {\n579\t\t\t// Try moving each node slightly\n580\t\t\tfor i, node := range bestSet.All() {\n581\t\t\t\t// Generate candidate position\n582\t\t\t\tcandidatePos := Point{\n583\t\t\t\t\tX: node.Position.X + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0, // ±0.5m\n584\t\t\t\t\tY: node.Position.Y + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0,\n585\t\t\t\t\tZ: node.Position.Z + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*0.5, // Less Z variation\n586\t\t\t\t}\n587\t\n588\t\t\t\t// Keep within bounds\n589\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.X = math.Max(minX, math.Min(maxX, candidatePos.X))\n590\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.Y = math.Max(minY, math.Min(maxY, candidatePos.Y))\n591\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.Z = math.Max(0, math.Min(maxZ, candidatePos.Z))\n592\t\n593\t\t\t\t// Create test set with this node moved\n594\t\t\t\ttestSet := NewNodeSet()\n595\t\t\t\tfor j, n := range bestSet.All() {\n596\t\t\t\t\tif j == i {\n597\t\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.AddVirtualNode(n.ID, n.Name, candidatePos)\n598\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n599\t\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.Add(n)\n600\t\t\t\t\t}\n601\t\t\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\t\t\t// Evaluate\n604\t\t\t\ttestLinks := GenerateAllLinks(testSet)\n605\t\t\t\ttestGDOP := NewGDOPComputer(testLinks, GridConfig{\n606\t\t\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n607\t\t\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n608\t\t\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n609\t\t\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n610\t\t\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n611\t\t\t\t})\n612\t\t\t\ttestResults := testGDOP.ComputeAll()\n613\t\t\t\ttestScore := testGDOP.CoverageScore(testResults)\n614\t\n615\t\t\t\t// Keep if better\n616\t\t\t\tif testScore > bestScore {\n617\t\t\t\t\tbestScore = testScore\n618\t\t\t\t\tbestSet.All()[i].Position = candidatePos\n619\t\t\t\t}\n620\t\t\t}\n621\t\t}\n622\t\n623\t\treturn bestSet\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// GenerateShoppingList creates a shopping list from simulation results\n627\tfunc GenerateShoppingList(space *Space, currentNodes *NodeSet) *ShoppingList {\n628\t\tnodes := currentNodes\n629\t\tif nodes == nil || nodes.Count() == 0 {\n630\t\t\tnodes = SuggestedNodes(space, 4)\n631\t\t}\n632\t\n633\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n634\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n635\t\n636\t\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n637\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n638\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n639\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n640\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n641\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n642\t\t})\n643\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n644\t\n645\t\tcoverage := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n646\t\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n647\t\n648\t\treturn &ShoppingList{\n649\t\t\tMinimumNodes:     MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n650\t\t\tRecommendedNodes: nodes.Count(),\n651\t\t\tExpectedAccuracy: ExpectedAccuracy(avgGDOP),\n652\t\t\tCoveragePercent:  coverage,\n653\t\t\tOptimalPositions: extractNodePositions(nodes),\n654\t\t}\n655\t}\n656\t\n657\tfunc extractNodePositions(nodes *NodeSet) []Point {\n658\t\tpositions := make([]Point, 0, nodes.Count())\n659\t\tfor _, n := range nodes.All() {\n660\t\t\tpositions = append(positions, n.Position)\n661\t\t}\n662\t\treturn positions\n663\t}\n664\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"eecb8034-1f22-4ebd-a931-2e1a46645cf1","uuid":"5af13aec-1b52-47f6-b51c-0b239b501946","timestamp":"2026-05-06T02:59:40.824Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/simulator/gdop.go","content":"package simulator\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\tmrand \"math/rand\"\n)\n\n// GDOPResult contains GDOP computation results for a single cell\ntype GDOPResult struct {\n\tX, Y, Z      float64  // Cell center position\n\tGDOP         float64  // Computed GDOP value (Infinity = no coverage)\n\tQuality      string   // \"excellent\", \"good\", \"fair\", \"poor\", \"none\"\n\tContributingLinks []string // Link IDs that contributed to this cell\n}\n\n// GridConfig defines the GDOP computation grid\ntype GridConfig struct {\n\tCellSize    float64 // Grid cell size in meters\n\tMinX, MinY  float64 // Grid origin\n\tWidth       float64 // Grid width\n\tDepth       float64 // Grid depth\n}\n\n// GDOPComputer computes Geometric Dilution of Precision for coverage analysis\ntype GDOPComputer struct {\n\tlinks     []Link\n\tconfig    GridConfig\n\tmaxZone   int // Maximum Fresnel zone to consider (default 3)\n}\n\n// NewGDOPComputer creates a new GDOP computer\nfunc NewGDOPComputer(links []Link, config GridConfig) *GDOPComputer {\n\tif config.CellSize <= 0 {\n\t\tconfig.CellSize = 0.2 // Default 20cm\n\t}\n\treturn &GDOPComputer{\n\t\tlinks:   links,\n\t\tconfig:  config,\n\t\tmaxZone: 3, // Default: consider first 3 Fresnel zones\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetMaxZone sets the maximum Fresnel zone to consider\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) SetMaxZone(zone int) {\n\tif zone < 1 {\n\t\tzone = 1\n\t}\n\tgc.maxZone = zone\n}\n\n// ComputeAll computes GDOP for the entire grid\n// Returns a slice of GDOP results indexed by cell position\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAll() [][]GDOPResult {\n\tnx := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Width / gc.config.CellSize))\n\tny := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Depth / gc.config.CellSize))\n\n\tresults := make([][]GDOPResult, ny)\n\n\tfor iy := 0; iy < ny; iy++ {\n\t\tresults[iy] = make([]GDOPResult, nx)\n\t\tfor ix := 0; ix < nx; ix++ {\n\t\t\tx := gc.config.MinX + (float64(ix)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n\t\t\ty := gc.config.MinY + (float64(iy)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n\t\t\tz := 1.0 // Use 1m height for 2D GDOP analysis\n\n\t\t\tresult := gc.ComputeAt(x, y, z)\n\t\t\tresults[iy][ix] = result\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn results\n}\n\n// ComputeAt computes GDOP at a specific point\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAt(x, y, z float64) GDOPResult {\n\tpoint := Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: z}\n\n\t// Collect links that cover this point (within maxZone Fresnel zones)\n\tvar coveringLinks []Link\n\tvar linkIDs []string\n\n\tfor _, link := range gc.links {\n\t\tif IsInFresnelZones(link.TX.Position, link.RX.Position, point, gc.maxZone) {\n\t\t\tcoveringLinks = append(coveringLinks, link)\n\t\t\tlinkIDs = append(linkIDs, link.TX.ID+\":\"+link.RX.ID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := GDOPResult{\n\t\tX:                 x,\n\t\tY:                 y,\n\t\tZ:                 z,\n\t\tContributingLinks: linkIDs,\n\t}\n\n\tif len(coveringLinks) < 2 {\n\t\t// Need at least 2 links for 2D localization\n\t\tresult.GDOP = math.Inf(1)\n\t\tresult.Quality = \"none\"\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute GDOP using angular diversity\n\tgdop := gc.computeGDOPAngular(point, coveringLinks)\n\tresult.GDOP = gdop\n\tresult.Quality = gdopToQuality(gdop)\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// computeGDOPAngular computes GDOP based on angular diversity of link directions\n// This is the 2D GDOP formula from the plan\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) computeGDOPAngular(point Point, links []Link) float64 {\n\t// Step 1: Collect link angles\n\t// For each link, compute the angle of the line from TX to RX as seen from point\n\ttype linkAngle struct {\n\t\ttheta float64 // angle in radians\n\t\tlink  Link\n\t}\n\n\tangles := make([]linkAngle, 0, len(links))\n\tfor _, link := range links {\n\t\t// Project to floor plane (ignore Z for 2D analysis)\n\t\tdx := link.RX.Position.X - link.TX.Position.X\n\t\tdy := link.RX.Position.Y - link.TX.Position.Y\n\t\ttheta := math.Atan2(dy, dx)\n\t\tangles = append(angles, linkAngle{theta: theta, link: link})\n\t}\n\n\tif len(angles) < 2 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Build Fisher information matrix\n\t// F = Σ [ [cos²(θ),       cos(θ)·sin(θ)],\n\t//        [cos(θ)·sin(θ), sin²(θ)       ] ]\n\tvar f00, f01, f11 float64\n\n\tfor _, la := range angles {\n\t\tc := math.Cos(la.theta)\n\t\ts := math.Sin(la.theta)\n\n\t\tf00 += c * c\n\t\tf01 += c * s\n\t\tf11 += s * s\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 3: Compute determinant\n\tdet := f00*f11 - f01*f01\n\n\t// Check for degenerate geometry (collinear links)\n\tif det <= 1e-6 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 4: Compute trace of F^-1\n\t// For 2x2 matrix: trace(F^-1) = (f00 + f11) / det\n\ttraceFInv := (f00 + f11) / det\n\n\t// Step 5: GDOP = sqrt(trace(F^-1))\n\tgdop := math.Sqrt(traceFInv)\n\n\treturn gdop\n}\n\n// gdopToQuality converts GDOP value to quality string\nfunc gdopToQuality(gdop float64) string {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn \"none\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n\t\treturn \"excellent\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n\t\treturn \"good\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n\t\treturn \"fair\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"poor\"\n}\n\n// CoverageScore computes the percentage of cells with \"good\" or better coverage\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) CoverageScore(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\n\tgoodCells := 0\n\ttotalCells := 0\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\ttotalCells++\n\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"excellent\" || cell.Quality == \"good\" {\n\t\t\t\tgoodCells++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn 100.0 * float64(goodCells) / float64(totalCells)\n}\n\n// AverageGDOP computes the average GDOP over all cells (excluding infinity)\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) AverageGDOP(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n\tsum := 0.0\n\tcount := 0\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(cell.GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tsum += cell.GDOP\n\t\t\t\tcount++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif count == 0 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\treturn sum / float64(count)\n}\n\n// QualityCounts returns the count of cells by quality level\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) QualityCounts(results [][]GDOPResult) map[string]int {\n\tcounts := map[string]int{\n\t\t\"excellent\": 0,\n\t\t\"good\":      0,\n\t\t\"fair\":      0,\n\t\t\"poor\":      0,\n\t\t\"none\":      0,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tcounts[cell.Quality]++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn counts\n}\n\n// FindDeadZones returns positions where coverage is \"none\" or \"poor\"\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) FindDeadZones(results [][]GDOPResult) []Point {\n\tdeadZones := make([]Point, 0)\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"none\" || cell.Quality == \"poor\" {\n\t\t\t\tdeadZones = append(deadZones, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn deadZones\n}\n\n// RecommendNodePosition suggests optimal positions for additional nodes\n// based on current dead zones\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) RecommendNodePosition(results [][]GDOPResult, space *Space) Point {\n\t// Find the centroid of the largest dead zone\n\tdeadZones := gc.FindDeadZones(results)\n\tif len(deadZones) == 0 {\n\t\t// No dead zones, suggest center of space\n\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\t\treturn Point{\n\t\t\tX: (minX + maxX) / 2,\n\t\t\tY: (minY + maxY) / 2,\n\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Cluster dead zones and find the largest cluster\n\t// Simplified: just return the centroid of all dead zones\n\tvar sumX, sumY float64\n\tfor _, dz := range deadZones {\n\t\tsumX += dz.X\n\t\tsumY += dz.Y\n\t}\n\n\tcentroid := Point{\n\t\tX: sumX / float64(len(deadZones)),\n\t\tY: sumY / float64(len(deadZones)),\n\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement for better coverage\n\t}\n\n\treturn centroid\n}\n\n// MinimumNodeCount estimates the minimum number of nodes needed for good coverage\n// Based on space dimensions and desired quality threshold\nfunc MinimumNodeCount(space *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n\twidth, depth, _ := space.Dimensions()\n\tarea := width * depth\n\n\t// Heuristic: nodes needed based on area and desired GDOP\n\t// For GDOP < 4 (good coverage): approximately 1 node per 15-20 m²\n\tif targetGDOP < 2 {\n\t\t// Excellent coverage requires more nodes\n\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 15.0))\n\t}\n\tif targetGDOP < 4 {\n\t\t// Good coverage\n\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 20.0))\n\t}\n\t// Fair coverage\n\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0))\n}\n\n// ExpectedAccuracy estimates the expected localization accuracy at a point\n// based on its GDOP value\nfunc ExpectedAccuracy(gdop float64) float64 {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Based on research: typical CSI accuracy with 4+ nodes is ±0.5-1.0m\n\t// GDOP < 2: ±0.5m, GDOP 2-4: ±1.0m, GDOP > 4: degrades further\n\tbaseAccuracy := 0.5 // meters for GDOP = 1\n\n\treturn baseAccuracy * gdop\n}\n\n// GDOPColor represents a color for GDOP visualization\ntype GDOPColor struct {\n\tR, G, B uint8 // RGB values 0-255\n}\n\n// GDOPColorMap returns the color for a given GDOP value for visualization\n// Uses: green (excellent), yellow (good), orange (fair), red (poor), gray (none)\nfunc GDOPColorMap(gdop float64) GDOPColor {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 80, G: 80, B: 80} // Gray for no coverage\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 34, G: 197, B: 94} // Green (#22c65e) for excellent\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 193, B: 7} // Yellow (#ffc107) for good\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 146, B: 0} // Orange (#ff9200) for fair\n\t}\n\treturn GDOPColor{R: 220, G: 53, B: 69} // Red (#dc3545) for poor\n}\n\n// GDOPHeatmapData represents flattened GDOP data for frontend rendering\ntype GDOPHeatmapData struct {\n\tWidth      int         `json:\"width\"`       // Grid width (columns)\n\tDepth      int         `json:\"depth\"`       // Grid depth (rows)\n\tCellSize   float64     `json:\"cell_size\"`   // Cell size in meters\n\tOriginX    float64     `json:\"origin_x\"`    // Grid origin X\n\tOriginY    float64     `json:\"origin_y\"`    // Grid origin Y\n\tGDOPValues []float64   `json:\"gdop_values\"` // Flattened GDOP values (9999 = infinity)\n\tQualities  []string    `json:\"qualities\"`   // Flattened quality strings\n\tColors     [][]uint8   `json:\"colors\"`      // Flattened RGB colors [width*depth*3]\n\tAccuracyMap []float64   `json:\"accuracy_map\"` // Expected accuracy in meters per cell\n}\n\n// ToHeatmapData converts GDOP results to a heatmap-friendly format\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ToHeatmapData(results [][]GDOPResult) *GDOPHeatmapData {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn &GDOPHeatmapData{}\n\t}\n\n\tdepth := len(results)    // rows (Y)\n\twidth := len(results[0]) // cols (X)\n\ttotalCells := width * depth\n\n\tdata := &GDOPHeatmapData{\n\t\tWidth:       width,\n\t\tDepth:       depth,\n\t\tCellSize:    gc.config.CellSize,\n\t\tOriginX:     gc.config.MinX,\n\t\tOriginY:     gc.config.MinY,\n\t\tGDOPValues:  make([]float64, totalCells),\n\t\tQualities:   make([]string, totalCells),\n\t\tColors:      make([][]uint8, totalCells),\n\t\tAccuracyMap: make([]float64, totalCells),\n\t}\n\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tresult := results[y][x]\n\n\t\t\t// GDOP value (9999 for infinity)\n\t\t\tif math.IsInf(result.GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = 9999.0\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = result.GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Quality string\n\t\t\tdata.Qualities[idx] = result.Quality\n\n\t\t\t// RGB color\n\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(result.GDOP)\n\t\t\tdata.Colors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n\n\t\t\t// Expected accuracy\n\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = ExpectedAccuracy(result.GDOP)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn data\n}\n\n// ComputeAccuracyMap computes expected accuracy for each cell\n// Returns a 2D array of accuracy values in meters (infinity = no coverage)\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAccuracyMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]float64 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\taccuracyMap := make([][]float64, len(results))\n\tfor i := range results {\n\t\taccuracyMap[i] = make([]float64, len(results[i]))\n\t\tfor j := range results[i] {\n\t\t\taccuracyMap[i][j] = ExpectedAccuracy(results[i][j].GDOP)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn accuracyMap\n}\n\n// ComputeColorMap computes RGB colors for each cell for visualization\n// Returns a flattened array of RGB values [width*depth*3]\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeColorMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]uint8 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tdepth := len(results)\n\twidth := len(results[0])\n\ttotalCells := width * depth\n\n\tcolors := make([][]uint8, totalCells)\n\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(results[y][x].GDOP)\n\t\t\tcolors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn colors\n}\n\n// GetWorstCoverageCells returns the N cells with the worst GDOP values\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetWorstCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten all cells\n\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort by GDOP (descending, so worst first)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n\t\t\t// Handle infinity: infinity is worse than any finite value\n\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n\n\t\t\tvar swap bool\n\t\t\tif iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = false // i stays (infinity at top)\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = true // j is infinity, should be before i\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP > cells[i].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif swap {\n\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return top N worst cells\n\tif n > len(cells) {\n\t\tn = len(cells)\n\t}\n\treturn cells[:n]\n}\n\n// GetBestCoverageCells returns the N cells with the best GDOP values\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetBestCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten all cells\n\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort by GDOP (ascending, so best first)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n\t\t\t// Handle infinity: finite values are better than infinity\n\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n\n\t\t\tvar swap bool\n\t\t\tif !iInf && jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = false // i is finite, j is infinity, i is better\n\t\t\t} else if iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = true // i is infinity, j is finite, j should be before i\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP < cells[i].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif swap {\n\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return top N best cells\n\tif n > len(cells) {\n\t\tn = len(cells)\n\t}\n\treturn cells[:n]\n}\n\n// OptimizeNodePositions uses a greedy algorithm to find better node positions\n// for a given number of nodes within the space\nfunc OptimizeNodePositions(space *Space, numNodes int, iterations int) *NodeSet {\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\n\t// Start with corner positions\n\tbestSet := NewNodeSet()\n\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < numNodes; i++ {\n\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tcorners[i],\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Add random position (Go 1.20+ automatically seeds global generator)\n\t\t\tpos := Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n\t\t\t\tZ: mrand.Float64() * maxZ,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tpos,\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate initial links and compute coverage\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(bestSet)\n\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t})\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\tbestScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n\n\t// Iterative improvement\n\tfor iter := 0; iter < iterations; iter++ {\n\t\t// Try moving each node slightly\n\t\tfor i, node := range bestSet.All() {\n\t\t\t// Generate candidate position\n\t\t\tcandidatePos := Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: node.Position.X + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0, // ±0.5m\n\t\t\t\tY: node.Position.Y + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0,\n\t\t\t\tZ: node.Position.Z + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*0.5, // Less Z variation\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Keep within bounds\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.X = math.Max(minX, math.Min(maxX, candidatePos.X))\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.Y = math.Max(minY, math.Min(maxY, candidatePos.Y))\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.Z = math.Max(0, math.Min(maxZ, candidatePos.Z))\n\n\t\t\t// Create test set with this node moved\n\t\t\ttestSet := NewNodeSet()\n\t\t\tfor j, n := range bestSet.All() {\n\t\t\t\tif j == i {\n\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.AddVirtualNode(n.ID, n.Name, candidatePos)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.Add(n)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Evaluate\n\t\t\ttestLinks := GenerateAllLinks(testSet)\n\t\t\ttestGDOP := NewGDOPComputer(testLinks, GridConfig{\n\t\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\ttestResults := testGDOP.ComputeAll()\n\t\t\ttestScore := testGDOP.CoverageScore(testResults)\n\n\t\t\t// Keep if better\n\t\t\tif testScore > bestScore {\n\t\t\t\tbestScore = testScore\n\t\t\t\tbestSet.All()[i].Position = candidatePos\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn bestSet\n}\n\n// GenerateShoppingList creates a shopping list from simulation results\nfunc GenerateShoppingList(space *Space, currentNodes *NodeSet) *ShoppingList {\n\tnodes := currentNodes\n\tif nodes == nil || nodes.Count() == 0 {\n\t\tnodes = SuggestedNodes(space, 4)\n\t}\n\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\n\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t})\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\tcoverage := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n\n\treturn &ShoppingList{\n\t\tMinimumNodes:     MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n\t\tRecommendedNodes: nodes.Count(),\n\t\tExpectedAccuracy: ExpectedAccuracy(avgGDOP),\n\t\tCoveragePercent:  coverage,\n\t\tOptimalPositions: extractNodePositions(nodes),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc extractNodePositions(nodes *NodeSet) []Point {\n\tpositions := make([]Point, 0, nodes.Count())\n\tfor _, n := range nodes.All() {\n\t\tpositions = append(positions, n.Position)\n\t}\n\treturn positions\n}\n","numLines":664,"startLine":1,"totalLines":664}}}

dB loss for each material type\nfunc WallPenetrationLoss(m WallMaterial) float64 {\n\tswitch m {\n\tcase MaterialDrywall:\n\t\treturn 3.0\n\tcase MaterialBrick, MaterialConcrete:\n\t\treturn struct{ X, Y, Z float64 }\n\n// NewPoint creates a new 3D point\nfunc NewPoint(x, y, z float64) Point {\n\treturn Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: z}\n}\n\n// Distance returns Euclidean distance to another point\nfunc (p Point) Distance(to Point) float64 {\n\tdx := to.X - p.X\n\tdy := to.Y - p.Y\n\tdz := to.Z - p.Z\n\treturn math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n}\n\n// Vector returns the direction vector from p to to (normalized)\nfunc (p Point) Vector(to Point) Point {\n\td := p.Distance(to)\n\tif d < 1e-9 {\n\t\treturn Point{0, 0, 0}\n\t}\n\treturn Point{\n\t\tX: (to.X - p.X) / d,\n\t\tY: (to.Y - {\n52\t\tconst PL0 = 40.0 // dB at 1m reference\n53\t\tconst d0 = 1.0   // reference distance in meters\n54\t\tconst n = 2.0    // path loss exponent (free space)\n55\t\n56\t\tif distance < 0.01 cx, cy float64) float64 {\n81\t\t\treturn (bx-ax)*(cy-ay) - (by-ay)*(cx-ax)\n82\t\t}\n83\t\n84\t\to1 := ccw(x1, y1, x2, y2, wx1, wy1)\n85\t\to2 := ccw(x1, y1, x2, y2, wx2, wy2)\n86\t\to3 := ccw(wx1, wy1, wx2, wy2, x1, y1)\n87\t\to4 := ccw(wx1, wy1, wx2, wy2, x2, y2)\n88\t\n89\t\t// Check for intersection\n90\t\treturn o1*o2 < 0 && o3*o4 < 0\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\t// ComputeRSSI computes the RSSI in {\n106\t\t\trssi = -30\n107\t\t}\n108\t\n109\t\treturn int8(rssi)\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t// DeltaRMS computes the expected deltaRMS motion score\n113\t// when a walker is at the given position (vs empty highest in zone 1, decreases with zone number\n119\t\t// Zone 1: 0.15, Zone 2: 0.08, Zone 3: 0.04, Zone 4: 0.02, Zone 5+: 0.01\n120\t\tswitch zone {\n121\t\tcase 1:\n122\t\t\treturn 0.15\n123\t\tcase 2:\n124\t\t\treturn 0.08\n125\t\tcase 3:\n126\t\t\treturn 0.04\n127\t\tcase 4:\n128\t\t\treturn 0.02\n129\t\tdefault:\n130\t\t\treturn 0.01\n131\t\t}\n132\t}\n133\t\n134\t// GenerateIQPair generates a synthetic I/Q pair for a subcarrier\n135\t// with amplitude and phase, plus Gaussian noise\n136\tfunc (pm *PhysicsModel) GenerateIQPair(amplitude, phase float64) (int8, int8) {\n137\t\t// Generate Gaussian noise using Box-Muller transform\n138\t\tu1 := rand.Float64()\n139\t\tu2 := rand.Float64()\n140\t\tz0 := math.Sqrt(-2.0*math.Log(u1)) * math.Cos(2.0*math.Pi*u2)\n141\t\tz1 := math.Sqrt(-2.0*math.Log(u1)) * math.Sin(2.0*math.Pi*u2)\n142\t\n143\t\tnoiseI := z0 * pm.noiseSigma\n144\t\tnoiseQ := z1 * pm.noiseSigma\n145\t\n146\t\t// Convert to I/Q\n147\t\ti := amplitude*math.Cos(phase) + noiseI\n148\t\tq := amplitude*math.Sin(phase) + noiseQ\n149\t\n150\t\t// Clamp to int8 range [-127, 127]\n151\t\t// Note: We avoid -128 to prevent overflow issues\n152\t\tif i > 127 {\n153\t\t\ti = 127\n154\t\t}\n155\t\tif i < -127 {\n156\t\t\ti = -127\n157\t\t}\n158\t\tif q > 127 {\n159\t\t\tq = 127\n160\t\t}\n161\t\tif q < -127 {\n162\t\t\tq = -127\n163\t\t}\n164\t\n165\t\treturn int8(i), int8(q)\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t// GenerateSubcarrierCSI generates CSI data for all subcarriers\n169\tfunc (pm *PhysicsModel) GenerateSubcarrierCSI(tx, rx, walker Point, nSub int, frameNum int) []struct{ I, Q int8 } {\n170\t\tresult := make([]struct{ I, Q int8 }, nSub)\n171\t\n172\t\t// Base amplitude from deltaRMS\n173\t\tdeltaRMS := pm.DeltaRMS(tx, rx, walker)\n174\t\tamplitude := deltaRMS * 500.0 // Scale to reasonable I/Q range\n175\t\n176\t\tfor k := 0; k < nSub; k++ {\n177\t\t\t// Compute phase at this subcarrier\n178\t\t\tphase := pm.PhaseAtSubcarrier(tx, rx, walker, k, frameNum)\n179\t\n180\t\t\t// Add subcarrier-dependent amplitude variation\n181\t\t\t// Simulates frequency-selective fading\n182\t\t\tfreqFading := 0.8 + 0.4*math.Sin(2*math.Pi*float64(k)/16.0)\n183\t\t\tsubAmplitude := amplitude * freqFading\n184\t\n185\t\t\tresult[k].I, result[k].Q = pm.GenerateIQPair(subAmplitude, phase)\n186\t\t}\n187\t\n188\t\treturn result\n189\t}\n190\t\n191\t// PhaseAtSubcarrier computes phase for a given subcarrier index\n192\tfunc (pm *PhysicsModel) PhaseAtSubcarrier(tx, rx, walker Point, subcarrierIndex, frameNum int) float64 {\n193\t\t// Total path length (TX -> walker -> RX)\n194\t\td1 := tx.Distance(walker)\n195\t\td2 := walker.Distance(rx)\n196\t\ttotalDist := d1 + d2\n197\t\n198\t\t// Phase = 2π × k × Δf × (d / c) + temporal_variation\n199\t\tphase := 2*math.Pi*float64(subcarrierIndex)*SubcarrierSpacing*(totalDist/C)\n200\t\n201\t\t// Add small temporal variation for realism\n202\t\ttemporalPhase := 0.1 * math.Sin(2*math.Pi*float64(frameNum)/100.0)\n203\t\tphase += temporalPhase\n204\t\n205\t\t// Normalize to [-π, π]\n206\t\tfor phase > math.Pi {\n207\t\t\tphase -= 2 * math.Pi\n208\t\t}\n209\t\tfor phase < -math.Pi {\n210\t\t\tphase += 2 * math.Pi\n211\t\t}\n212\t\n213\t\treturn phase\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\t// ValidateRSSI validates that RSSI is within expected range for distance\n217\tfunc ValidateRSSI(rssi int8, distance float64) bool {\n218\t\t// Expected RSSI range for given distance\n219\t\texpectedPathLoss := 40.0 + 20.0*math.Log10(distance/1.0)\n220\t\texpectedRSSI := -30.0 - expectedPathLoss\n221\t\n222\t\t// Allow ±20 dB tolerance\n223\t\tminRSSI := expectedRSSI - 20.0\n224\t\tmaxRSSI := expectedRSSI + 20.0\n225\t\n226\t\t// Clamp to realistic bounds\n227\t\tif minRSSI < -90 {\n228\t\t\tminRSSI = -90\n229\t\t}\n230\t\tif maxRSSI > -30 {\n231\t\t\tmaxRSSI = -30\n232\t\t}\n233\t\n234\t\treturn float64(rssi) >= minRSSI && float64(rssi) <= maxRSSI\n235\t}\n236\t\n237\t// ValidateIQValues checks that I/Q values are in valid int8 range\n238\t// Note: int8 values are always in range, but we check for sensible CSI values\n239\tfunc ValidateIQValues(i, q int8) bool {\n240\t\treturn i >= -127 && q >= -127 // Upper bound implicit in int8 type\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// IsInFresnelZones checks if a point is within the first N Fresnel zones\n244\tfunc IsInFresnelZones(tx, rx, point Point, maxZone int) bool {\n245\t\tzone := FresnelZoneNumber(tx, rx, point)\n246\t\treturn zone <= maxZone && zone > 0\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\t// ComputeFresnelModulation computes the Fresnel zone modulation factor\n250\t// Returns a value between 0 and 1, where 1 is maximum modulation (zone 1)\n251\tfunc {\n256\t\t\treturn 1.0\n257\t\t}\n258\t\tif zone >= 5 {\n259\t\t\treturn 0.0\n260\t\t}\n261\t\n262\t\treturn 1.0 / math.Pow(float64(zone), 2.0)\n263\t}\n264\t\n265\t// ComputeLinkQuality estimates link quality (0-1) based on geometry\n266\t// Higher quality when links have good angular diversity\n267\tfunc ComputeLinkQuality(nodes []Point) float64 {\n268\t\tif len(nodes) < 2 {\n269\t\t\treturn 0.0\n270\t\t}\n271\t\n272\t\t// Simple metric: spread of node positions\n273\t\t// Compute centroid\n274\t\tvar cx, cy, cz float64\n275\t\tfor _, n := range nodes {\n276\t\t\tcx += n.X\n277\t\t\tcy += n.Y\n278\t\t\tcz += n.Z\n279\t\t}\n280\t\tcx /= float64(len(nodes))\n281\t\tcy /= float64(len(nodes))\n282\t\tcz /= float64(len(nodes))\n283\t\n284\t\t// Compute average distance from centroid\n285\t\tavgDist := 0.0\n286\t\tfor _, n := range nodes {\n287\t\t\tdx := n.X - cx\n288\t\t\tdy := n.Y - cy\n289\t\t\tdz := n.Z - cz\n290\t\t\tavgDist += math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n291\t\t}\n292\t\tavgDist /= float64(len(nodes))\n293\t\n294\t\t// Normalize: 5m spread = excellent quality (1.0)\n295\t\tquality := avgDist / 5.0\n296\t\tif quality > 1.0 {\n297\t\t\tquality =
